title: The AGE-RAGE-GLO1 Pathway in Glioma: A Systematic Review of Its Role in Tumour Biology and Therapy.
snippet: Gliomas, particularly glioblastoma (GB), are aggressive brain tumours with poor prognosis and limited therapeutic options. Chronic oxidative stress and inflammation are central to glioma pathogenesis, driven in part by the accumulation of Advanced Glycation End-products (AGEs) and their interaction with the receptor RAGE. The Glyoxalase I (GLO1) detoxifies....

title: Targeting 5-methylcytosine RNA methylation-mediated CX26/IκBα/NF-κB signaling by salvianolic acid B attenuates rheumatoid arthritis progression.
publicationName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
journalName: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
snippet: Developing novel therapeutic targets and innovative drugs for rheumatoid arthritis (RA) remains highly challenging. Despite the involvement of 5-methylcytosine (m5C) RNA methylation in various biological processes, its specific roles in RA and potential applications in drug development remain unclear.
